key_insight: Acoustoelastic Imaging reconstructs internal stress fields directly from shear wave propagation by solving an inverse wave-equation problem, avoiding explicit constitutive model fitting.
engineering_relevance: This enables non-destructive, high-resolution stress mapping in heterogeneous solids and soft tissues, with potential use in material inspection and biomedical elastography.
method: Measure full shear-wave fields under pre-existing stress.
Infer wave-equation coefficients with full waveform inversion and physics-informed learning.
Convert recovered coefficients into stress magnitude and principal directions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical simulations and ultrasound shear wave elastography experiments, achieving sub-wavelength resolution below 0.28λ and accurate reconstruction of nonuniform stress fields.
watch_item: Practical deployment may still depend on robust wave measurements and stable inversion in noisy, strongly heterogeneous, or poorly instrumented settings.
paper_title: Direct stress imaging from shear wave propagation
